insights.title: GET /todos missing requireAuth middleware
insights.rationale: POST /todos and DELETE /todos/:id are auth-protected but GET /todos is not, allowing unauthenticated users to read any user's todo list.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Add requireAuth middleware to GET /todos and DELETE /todos/:id to match POST /todos auth protection.

insights.title: DELETE /todos/:id missing requireAuth middleware
insights.rationale: DELETE /todos/:id has no applies_middleware edge to requireAuth, unlike POST /todos. Unauthenticated callers can delete arbitrary todos.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Apply requireAuth middleware to DELETE /todos/:id route.

insights.title: GET /health has no error handling middleware wired
insights.rationale: errorHandler exists but there is no edge from the health route or app bootstrap confirming it is applied globally, leaving unhandled errors possible on this route.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Wire errorHandler middleware globally in createApp after all route registrations.

insights.title: verifyToken called by both identifyUser and requireAuth — cache or deduplicate
insights.rationale: verifyToken (likely a JWT decode+verify) is invoked independently by two middleware functions on every request, doubling CPU and potentially I/O cost per request.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Add request-scoped caching to verifyToken so the result is computed once per request and reused by both identifyUser and requireAuth.

insights.title: signToken is a crypto utility living inside authService — extract it
insights.rationale: signToken and verifyToken are pure JWT utilities but are co-located with business-logic services (registerUser, loginUser), making them hard to reuse and test in isolation.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Extract signToken and verifyToken into a dedicated src/services/tokenService.ts module.

insights.title: toTodo row-mapper utility is buried inside todoRepo — extract it
insights.rationale: toTodo is a data-transformation helper, not a data-access function. Keeping it in todoRepo conflates mapping logic with DB access, hindering reuse and unit testing.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Extract toTodo into src/models/todo.ts or a dedicated mapper file and import it back into todoRepo.

insights.title: findUserByEmail called by both registerUser and loginUser without caching
insights.rationale: findUserByEmail hits the DB on every auth operation. A short-lived cache (e.g. per-request or TTL-based) would reduce redundant lookups during high-traffic login/register flows.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Wrap findUserByEmail with a short TTL cache to avoid duplicate DB hits within the same auth flow.

insights.title: createApp is a high-fanout bootstrap hub — consider splitting concerns
insights.rationale: createApp calls runMigrations, registers routers, and presumably wires middleware — mixing DB lifecycle, routing, and middleware setup in one function, making it a fragile change magnet.
insights.suggestedPrompt: Split createApp into separate initDb and registerRoutes helpers called from a thin createApp orchestrator.
